evidence: Build/check/test run clean on main post-merge under the project's pinned toolchain. Independent runtime demo (fresh scenarios, not the implementer's own test file) drove runWorkOrderLoop directly: interactive gate-decline yields aborted-by-human with no commit_sha; interactive gate-accept commits via _internals.attestedCommit using the gate-mutated event.input (confirmed the writer-identity mutation reaches the actual commit call, one ctx.ui.confirm invocation); non-interactive mode with a passing real-check yields completed-pending-commit, no commit attempted, zero confirm calls. A fresh Explore agent independently re-confirmed the ctx.ui.confirm sweep: exactly two call sites exist across pi-agent-dispatch/pi-workflows (work-order-loop.ts's human-OK gate, auth-gate.ts's own gate), both already hasUI-guarded -- no third site found, no fix needed beyond the routing change itself.

criteria_results.criterion: A non-interactive run-work-order-loop dispatch does not invoke, and is not refused on, any of the 8 pi-only gated tools
run-work-order-loop's commit is produced via the gated commit-attested tool path, not the ungated library call
npm run build, npm run check, npm test all pass clean; docs-surface-sync applied
